content.claim: Post-reform identification growth in Finland concentrated in the new 'intensified support' tier (rising from 2.8% in 2011 to 12.2% by 2020), while 'special support' (top tier) remained flat at approximately 8.5–9.0%, suggesting reclassification across a newly created category rather than uniform acceleration.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Observation applies only to post-reform period; pre-2010 trend comparison not provided
Tier distribution patterns could reflect genuine shift in need profiles or administrative redistribution
Hungarian comparable data lack equivalent tiered breakdown, preventing direct cross-national validation
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
